data.body:
=== DESIGN DOC: dso-dimension-catalog.md ===

# DSO review-dimension catalog (COMPLETE, overlaps preserved separately)

Consolidated from a 5-way chunked walkthrough of DSO (`~/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/`).
Every individual dimension is listed at its source even when it overlaps another — NO merging.
Format: **dim** — what it evaluates — `file:line` — where used — scoring.

Legend for scoring: `1-5` = 1-5+N/A rubric; `PASS/FAIL` etc = verdict enum; `gate` = boolean/threshold.

================================================================
## PART 1 — BRAINSTORM / EPIC-SCRUTINY (epic spec level)
================================================================

### 1.1 Fidelity reviewers (Step 4; pass = all dims ≥4; epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:311)
- self_contained — planner can decompose w/o clarifying Qs — agent-clarity.md:23 — 1-5
- success_measurable — every SC observable/pass-fail; post-sprint-only ⇒ score ≤2 — agent-clarity.md:24-28 — 1-5
- right_sized — coherent single-sprint unit; too-large/too-small signals — scope.md:23 — 1-5
- no_overlap — scope differentiated from other milestones — scope.md:24 — 1-5
- dependency_aware — prereq milestones named w/ reason — scope.md:25 — 1-5
- consumer_completeness — all Part-C consumers covered by SC or descoped (N/A if Part C skipped) — scope.md:26 — 1-5
- user_impact — named user + today's problem + observable improvement — value.md:24 — 1-5
- validation_signal — ≥1 SC has in-session-executable validation mechanism — value.md:25 — 1-5
- technical_feasibility — integrations verified (docs+working GitHub examples) — feasibility-reviewer.md:134-144 — 1-5 (conditional 4th)
- integration_risk — risk integration failures block/delay (higher=lower risk) — feasibility-reviewer.md:146-157 — 1-5; any dim <3 ⇒ FEASIBILITY_GAP (pipeline:313)
  - feasibility sub-checks: capability-gap class (verified/partial/unverified/contradicted) :107-112; CLI `--help` surface MATCH/MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED :60-67,175-188; auth HTTPS precondition :114-116; critical gap ⇒ spike :118-127

### 1.2 Inline scrutiny-pipeline checks (orchestrator self-review)
- Artifact-contradiction (Part A) — user-named artifacts missing from SCs (fuzzy) — epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:30-53 — gate
- Sync-loop (Part B) — bidirectional flow → dup/false-conflict/loop — :59 — finding
- Race-condition (Part B) — concurrent modify of shared state — :60 — finding
- Invalidated-assumptions (Part B) — new data breaks hashing/parse/cache/diff — :61 — finding
- Parsing-ambiguity (Part B) — user content contains format delimiters — :62 — finding
- Deprecation/rename reference sweep (Part B) — grep old paths + report count — :63 — finding
- Inference-Signal Scan (Part B) — hedging/confident trigger-words ⇒ MUST Read/Grep verify inferred input source — :65-84 — verified/gap
  - contract-doc ground-truth rule (read impl file, not just .md) :80; verification MANDATORY, cached context not a substitute :82
- Shared-Artifact Impact (Part C) — file-mode (2+ external consumers / move-rename-remove) + behavioral-prohibition mode; emits covered_by_SC tuples — :88-115 — feeds consumer_completeness
- Cross-Epic relates_to suggestion (Part C ext) — artifact overlaps other open epics (type=epic,status=open,exclude self+children+closed) — :117-157 — user-approval gate

### 1.3 Web-research warrant — 6 bright-line triggers (Step 2; :169-174)
external integration · unfamiliar dependency · security/auth/credentials · novel architectural pattern · performance/scalability · migration/compatibility — each boolean; + agent-judgment trigger :178; + user-request trigger :181

### 1.4 Scenario analysis (Step 3)
- complexity-scaling gate — ≥5 SC or integration ⇒ full; 3-4 SC no-integration ⇒ cap 3; ≤2 SC ⇒ skip — :236-240
- Red-team categories (scenario-red-team.md): Runtime (timeouts/race/out-of-order/concurrent) :17-24 · Deployment (migration-conflict/first-setup/env/CI-CD) :27-33 · Configuration (misuse/invalid/missing-default/boundary) :36-42 · Assumption (hidden-precondition/user-behavior/causal/architectural) :45-58 — severity critical/high/medium/low
- Blue-team filters (ALL must pass): Possible :20-25 · Actionable :28-32 · Distinct :35-40 · High-Confidence :42-47 — accept/reject, fail-open

### 1.5 SC drafting rules + verifiable-sc-check (Phase 2)
- SC count 3-6 :549 · verifiable pass/fail :550 · session-verifiable :551 · outcomes-not-impl :553 · ≥1 validation-signal hint :554 · executable-artifact end-to-end SC :555 · superseding-not-an-SC :556
- Context-narrative: name user :561 / today's problem :562 / no jargon :564
- State-Lifecycle Owner block — CREATE/UPDATE/CONSUME/RETIRE owners; unowned RETIRE = defect — :579-589
- Verify-Intent 3-element (Subject+Action+Observable) — verifiable-sc-check.md:78-86
- Session-feasibility self-check — verifiable-sc-check.md:11-30
- End-state-only litmus (route transitions to Closure Checks) — verifiable-sc-check.md:50-66
- Closure-Check one-shot-verifiable — epic-description-template.md:21-31

### 1.6 Other brainstorm gates
- Scope-bullet validity (no "verify whether/check if/TBD/...") — SKILL.md:244
- Anti-redundancy semantic-duplicate :21-27 · codebase-discoverability :190-192 · one-question HARD-GATE :198-200 · compression anti-pattern :296
- Completeness Attestation exhausted/open :300-307 · Intent Gap Analysis :276
- UI: keyword-trigger (≥3/0/1-2) ui-keyword-trigger.md:6-11 · intent classifier ui/non-ui · UX probes (criticality/non_happy_path/flow_entry_exit) ux-probe-set.md:9-29 · UI-copy 9-signal scan SKILL.md:364-377
- value-effort-scorer: value 1-5 :39-45 · effort 1-5 :51-59 · priority P0-P4 matrix :67-103
- Follow-on readiness (placeholder/inference signals) follow-on-epic-gate.md:42-55
- Architectural-probe gate (## Self-Use Compatibility) SKILL.md:182-187; epic class architectural/integration/infra/behavioral :165
- Platform-capability probes (auto-merge/branch-protection/required-checks/merge-methods) :337-343 + contradiction gate approval-gate.md:28-47
- Bot-psychologist (Step 5, dispatched): 17-point LLM failure taxonomy + 5 RCA probes — bot-psychologist.md:53-79

================================================================
## PART 2 — PREPLANNING (story level)
================================================================

### 2.1 Architecture Board — 6 reviewers, ALWAYS-ON w/ conditional findings, pass_threshold:4
- Security: access_classification (security.md:23) · data_protection (:24)
- Performance: latency (:24) · resource_efficiency (:25) · scalability (:26)
- Accessibility: wcag_compliance (:24) · inclusive_ux (:25)
- Testing: user_journey_coverage (:25) · boundary_scenarios (:26) · verifiable_outcomes (:27)
- Reliability: error_handling (:23) · failover (:24) · [blast_radius tiebreaker, only LOWERS :46-53]
- Maintainability: coupling_risk (:24) · changeability (:25) · documentation/ADR (:26)

### 2.2 Story-decomposer (opus) + orchestrator re-checks
- sc_coverage_plan (per-SC fully/partial/uncovered/out-of-scope) SKILL.md:447; story-decomposer.md:199-208
- DD→SC traceability (← Satisfies: sc-N) :448 · no dangling deps :450 · INVEST story-decomposer.md:229 · 2-5 measurable DDs :233
- SC-contradiction check (DD achieved but SC still unsatisfied) SKILL.md:880
- DD-quality: TDD Done-of-Done :895 · compound " AND " :916 · vague "properly/correctly/should" :916 · spec-fidelity ≥80% word-match :938 · scoping-assumption Verify: :1304-1310

### 2.3 Phase E adversarial — red-team story_review (red-team-reviewer.md)
- SC→DD coverage audit (fully/partial/uncovered/out-of-scope; 3-condition standard) :156-171
- 8-category interaction taxonomy: implicit shared state :190 · conflicting assumptions :198 · dependency gaps :206 · scope overlaps :213 · ordering/sequencing :220 · consumer impact/operational readiness :227 · residual references :235
- inference_challenge mode (inferred-vs-explicit; sampling tiers) :46-64
- blue-team-filter: actionable/real-interaction/distinct/high-confidence — blue-team-filter.md:30-57 — accept/reject

### 2.4 INVEST + slicing (Phase F)
INVEST (Small = one sub-agent session) SKILL.md:650 · vertical slice :654 · foundation/enhancement split :670

================================================================
## PART 3 — IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN (task level)
================================================================

### 3.1 Architectural reviewers (Step 2, pass_threshold:4) — only if new pattern
- Best-Practices: modern_standards (best-practices.md:24) · simplicity [helper at 3+ consumers] (:25) · testability [≤2 mocked collaborators] (:26)
- Project-Alignment: pattern_consistency [5 invariants] (project-alignment.md:24) · convention_compliance [4 invariants] (:25)
- Justification: divergence_justified [name alternative + concrete limitation + measurable advantage] (justification.md:24)

### 3.2 Plan committee — 5 reviewers, pass_threshold:5 (ALL must =5), 18 dims
- Task-Design: atomicity [3 gates] (task-design.md:23) · acceptance_criteria (:24) · verify_command_robustness [8 defect classes, never-null] (:25)
- TDD (7 dims): tdd_discipline :80 · test_isolation :81 · red_green_sequence :82 · test_boundary_coverage :83 · red_test_dependency :84 · exemption_justification :85 · bidirectional_test_coverage :86 [+ instruction-file scoring adjustments :68-74]
- Safety: incremental_deploy (safety.md:24) · backward_compat [expand-contract] (:25)
- Dependencies: dag_validity [valid DAG, critical path] (dependencies.md:23) · no_coupling [no shared-mutable race] (:24)
- Completeness: criteria_coverage (completeness.md:23) · e2e_coverage (:24) · ac_semantic_consistency (:25) · dd_collective_ac_coverage [never-null, 5-step audit] (:26)
- conflict patterns scanned: expand_vs_reduce/more_vs_less/strict_vs_flexible/add_vs_remove/boundary_vs_coverage/red_green_vs_atomicity — review-criteria.md:67-74

### 3.3 Proposal generation gates (Step 1, approach-proposer)
- distinctness 4 axes (data layer/control flow/dependency graph/interface boundary) SKILL.md:374-377
- complexity-gate Gates 1-3 per proposal (YAGNI/Rule-of-Three/Dependency) — approach-proposer.md:97-103
- min 3 proposals :370 · 6 schema fields + risk low/med/high proposal-schema.md:47-74
- Fitness-function-as-scaffolding (baseline capture) approach-proposer.md:117-125

### 3.4 Approach-decision-maker (opus) — 5 scored dims + anti-patterns
- Codebase Alignment :49 · Blast Radius :63 · Testability :77 · Simplicity [anchored on complexity-gate] :89 · Robustness :101 (all 1-5)
- Context-hierarchy disqualifiers: epic SC non-negotiable / story DD required / considerations advisory :33-43
- 7 anti-patterns: golden hammer · premature abstraction · cargo cult · resume-driven · premature optimization · NIH · config-surface proliferation :115-127

### 3.5 Gap-analysis (Step 6, opus, COMPLEX only) — gap-analysis.md
6 categories: race :27 · state/file conflict :34 · implicit assumption :42 · missing error/rollback :50 · cross-task interference :59 · AC verify defects [9 sub-classes] :67-78

### 3.6 Step-1 scans + thresholds
- Ambiguity scan 6 signals (undefined scope/implicit AC/conflicting/missing persona/unstated constraints/ambiguous priority) SKILL.md:290-297
- Unsatisfiable-SC 3 checks ⇒ REPLAN_ESCALATE :301-308
- Doc-only skip gate 3 signals :359-362
- Cross-cutting: ≥3 layers OR ≥5 interfaces/classes ⇒ Step 2 :334-342
- Testing-mode RED/GREEN/UPDATE + test-classification (needs-creation/modification/removal/still-valid) :723-776
- Behavioral coverage cross-check (Step 5b, per-DD PASS/FAIL; 13 FAIL-signal commands) :1312-1332

### 3.7 task-decomposer
3-gate granularity :131-135 · verify-command robustness [11-item checklist] :174-188 · DD partition invariant :121-127

### 3.8 complexity-evaluator (haiku) — 8 dims (agents/complexity-evaluator.md)
1 Files (≤1/2-3/>3) :80 · 2 Layers (≤1/2/≥3) :90 · 3 Interfaces (≥1⇒COMPLEX) :103 · 4 scope_certainty [type-forked bug vs story/epic; Low⇒COMPLEX] :112-154 · 5 Confidence [Medium⇒COMPLEX] :156 · 6 Blast Radius [complex_override] :165 · 7 Pattern Familiarity :176 · 8 External Boundary Count :186
- promotion rules :200-206; epic-only overrides (multi-persona/UI+backend/migration/foundation-enhancement/external-integration/>6 SC + done-def + single-concern) :224-253; manifest_depth :210-220; feasibility_review_recommended :257-264

### 3.9 complexity-gate — 6 gates (complexity-gate.md)
Gate1 YAGNI :26 · Gate2 Rule-of-Three [3+ call sites] :40 · Gate3 Dependency [≤30 lines or correctness/security] :50 · Gate4 Scale Threshold :61 · Gate5 Profiling-First :74 · Gate6 LLM Self-Audit :86 · Sandbagging Prohibition :108

================================================================
## PART 4 — COHERENCE-WALK + PLAN-REVIEW GATE  *** (missed by first pass) ***
================================================================

### 4.1 coherence-walk shared rubric (verdict-rubric.md) — applies to ALL chunks
- Verdict: **PASS / AMBIGUOUS / FAIL** (3-valued!) — :21-48
- *** Cross-cutting reviewer DISCIPLINES (the anti-false-positive machinery) ***:
  - Single-question scoping — judge ONLY the chunk's narrow question; exclude unrelated findings — :11-15
  - Cite-or-omit — no file:line ⇒ don't make the claim ("findings without evidence are change-detector noise → false positives that block legitimate migrations") — :61-63
  - Refuse-to-invent / no-speculation — only judge text actually read; missing file = citable absence — :65-67
  - Severity: critical (wrong runtime outcome) :51 / important (real gap, not every run) :52 / minor (doc drift) :53
- AMBIGUOUS = cannot conclusively determine (dual paths/partial); prefer AMBIGUOUS+question over FAIL+hand-wave

### 4.2 coherence-walk chunks (each a 4-6 part conjunction; reviews SC vs Closure-Check separation per stage)
- Chunk A brainstorm: template separation / end-state litmus present / refusal copy / approval-gate well-formed — chunk-a-brainstorm.md:24-30
- Chunk B preplanning: SC inheritance partitioned / end-state DD semantics / distinction preserved / annotation discipline — chunk-b-preplanning.md:20-26
- Chunk C impl-plan: task-AC excludes closure checks / no conflation / no reverse propagation / AC-library distinguishes — chunk-c-implementation-plan.md:20-26
- Chunk D sprint: verifier reads closure-checks separately / parent_id-only walk / closed-deleted skip / legacy-absent=0 / story-vs-epic dispatch / BLOCK-vs-WARN — chunk-d-sprint.md:20-30
- Chunk E commit-review: reviewers don't author closure checks / review-PASS≠closure-pass / distinct failure surfaces / commit doesn't mutate — chunk-e-commit-and-review.md:26-32
- Chunk F operational-dry-run: classifier yield / mis-classification spot-check / no epic ≥50% uncertain — chunk-f-operational-dry-run.md:33-37

### 4.3 plan-review rubric (plan-review-dispatch.md) — 4 dims, 1-5, VERDICT PASS/REVISE
- Feasibility [buildable? missing steps? tools exist? hidden deps?] :12-16
- Completeness [error/edge cases, testing strategy, integration points] :18-22
- YAGNI/Overengineering [+ config-surface check] :24-29
- Codebase Alignment [conventions, established patterns, accurate refs] :31-35
- "find real problems, not nitpicks" framing :2

### 4.4 plan-review SKILL + gate hook *** (DIRECT GATE PRECEDENT) ***
- PASS = all 4 ≥4; REVISE = any <4 — plan-review/SKILL.md:74,79
- artifact_type branch: design→opus, implementation_plan→sonnet — :38-54
- schema validation pre-gate (hash) :58-70 · severity-ordered remediation (one pass) :80-86 · marker file plan-review-status :88-104
- **plan-review-gate.sh** — PreToolUse hook on **ExitPlanMode**: checks marker exists + first line == `passed`; exit 2 = BLOCK; **FAILS OPEN** on non-repo / internal error (ERR trap); NO force-flag; bypass = run the review skill. (We diverge: fail-CLOSED, bind to tickets commit, fire on claim not ExitPlanMode.)

================================================================
## PART 5 — completion-verifier (THE CLOSE GATE — our mirror) + remediation
================================================================

### 5.1 completion-verifier (agents/completion-verifier.md) — SC(epic)/DD(story) PASS/FAIL
- per-criterion: observable-behavior [needs execution trace; inspection ⇒ FAIL] vs documented-behavior [Grep/Read OK] :212-216
- Aspirational-implementation 5-signal detector (any 2 ⇒ FAIL): RED stubs / no live-path callers / competing impl / doc contradictions / missing integration artifacts :238-245
- Descendant subtree walk: **parent_id ONLY**, skip closed/deleted, blocked=open-unless-terminal, **open-descendant Closure-Checks BLOCK vs WARN** :160-203
- failure_category: external_blocker / internal_architecture_gap / evidence_pending :218-221
- project closure gates SC9 (coverage)/SC13 (restart)/SC14 (FP<10%) :259-326

### 5.2 verification-remediation-planner (FAIL → what to do) — 4-branch tree
replan_story (in-story zero-evidence) :54 · new_tasks_in_story (all-partial; implement vs legitimize_marker) :64-90 · new_story_in_epic :92 · replan_epic (cross-epic) :105 — confidence HIGH/MED/LOW

### 5.3 remediation-loop-protocol
oscillation-check at cycle≥2; OSCILLATION_HALT on recurring findings; MAX_CYCLES=3 — remediation-loop-protocol.md:52-66

================================================================
## PART 6 — CODE-REVIEWER FAMILY (post-execution; OUT OF SCOPE for plan gate
##          but their anti-FP disciplines are reusable guardrails)
================================================================
- Verify-Before-Assert (7 sub-prohibitions on unverified claims) — code-reviewer-deep-arch.md:89-108
- Severity calibration MINOR floor (6 things that are minor-not-important) :265-274
- NOT-Flag auto-downgrade (7 patterns) :333-353 · Behavioral-testing VALID patterns (7, don't flag) :373-399
- arbiter BLOCK/DEFER/DROP + cross_reviewer_agreement(4)/impact_class(8-floor) — code-reviewer-arbiter.md:40-136
- bloat-blue-team: confidence-blind classification, asymmetric error (default DISMISS), NEEDS_HUMAN ≤20%/≥80% — bloat-blue-team.md:23-69
- scope-drift-reviewer: creation-vs-restoration, in_scope/ambiguous/out_of_scope — scope-drift-reviewer.md:99-122
- code-reviewer-verifier / second-source-verifier: fail-open absence-claim verification

================================================================
## SYNTHESIS NOTES FOR OUR REBAR GATE
================================================================
1. **3-valued verdict** (PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL) from coherence-walk fits our block/advise split perfectly:
   FAIL→block, AMBIGUOUS→surface-but-overridable (advisory), PASS→clear. Better than binary.
2. **Anti-FP disciplines** (cite-or-omit, no-speculation, single-question scoping) = the direct lever for
   Joe's "Sonnet can answer reliably without false positives." Bake into EVERY reviewer prompt.
3. **plan-review-gate.sh is the closest existing gate** — but fails OPEN; we fail CLOSED + bind to commit.
4. **Severity tiers tied to runtime impact** (critical/important/minor) — adopt for finding triage.
5. **verification-remediation-planner's 4-branch tree** maps to our FAIL outcomes (fix plan / add tasks /
   decompose into children / escalate).
6. **completion-verifier (close gate) is our mirror** — reuse: parent_id-only child walk, observable-vs-
   documented split, aspirational-implementation detector (the inverse: at plan time, detect aspirational PLAN).
7. DSO's per-skill staging (brainstorm→preplanning→impl-plan) generalizes to has_children + complexity;
   type only carries unique meaning in scope_certainty (bug repro/fix-scope fork) and docs-only TDD exemption.
